name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
resistance_profile: Hardest at the top of the arc with arms out at shoulder height; the dumbbells go nearly weightless in the bottom (stretched) position.
cues: Lead with the pinkies, out and back
Squeeze the rear delts, don't shrug
execution_steps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor.
Let the dumbbells hang under your shoulders with a slight, fixed bend in your elbows.
Raise the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc, leading with your pinkies, until your arms reach shoulder height.
Squeeze your rear delts at the top, then lower under control without shrugging or swinging.
good_when: You only have dumbbells and want to directly isolate the rear delts.
You want a simple accessory to bolt onto a pull day or shoulder day.
bad_when: You cannot keep your torso still — a chest-supported or cable version holds tension more consistently.
You want load in the stretched position — the dumbbells go nearly weightless at the bottom.
